sglang.title: fix: dot-boundary match in is_layer_skipped for FP8 modules_to_not_convert
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

is_layer_skipped uses naive substring match (ignored in prefix) on modules_to_not_convert entries, which silently fires when an entry is a prefix-substring of a fused linear name.
Fix replaces the match with a dot-boundary comparison and adds a fallback fused-shards map so the behavior is p

sglang.title: Skip mamba_pool_idx revert for session requests in _get_new_batch_prefill_raw
sglang.author: shenxiul
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The revert path added in #21404 frees session-held mamba slots when a request can't be added to a batch, causing double-free when the session later closes (we observed mamba_leaked=-9 on our workload).
A streaming session request has its mamba_pool_idx restored from a SessionSlot via slot.re

Fix /generate endpoint crash when sampling params contain null values (e.g., {"top_p": null}).

TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'fl

Unpack from awq format

Validate an unmerged PR on GB200 hardware.
Test a fork PR — fork contributors had no path to exercise their changes on the shared GB200 cluster.
Run a targ

sglang.title: Dual MoE CUDA graph capture for lora/nolora batches
sglang.author: sshleifer
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
When LoRA is enabled with a triton MoE backend, capture two sets of CUDA graphs per batch size: one with LoRA kernels recorded and one without. At replay time, batches without active adapters use the faster nolora graph, avoiding LoRA kernel overhead entirely. Controlled by --record-nolora-g

sglang.title: [LoRA] Fix EP + per-expert MoE LoRA illegal memory access
sglang.author: jybsuper
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Two related bugs prevent --tp N --ep M --enable-lora --lora-backend triton --moe-runner-backend triton from running on any MoE model with per-expert LoRA adapters; both reliably crash in the first forward pass with CUDA error: an illegal memory access was encountered. This PR fixes both and

sglang.title: fix retrive -> retrieve typo
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Rename retrive -> retrieve on the Python side. Kernel schema (sgl-kernel) is untouched; at the kernel-call boundary the kwarg LHS is kept as retrive_* to match the existing op signature.

The *-low-disk label is advertised by both the existing large-disk B200 runners and the new low-disk runners, so this job can now land

vllm.title: [Bugfix][CPU][RISC-V] Clamp exp() input to prevent NaN
vllm.author: lyd1992
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FP32Vec16::exp() in cpu_types_riscv_impl.hpp produced NaN for large inputs because -inf * 0.0 = NaN in IEEE 754 after the polynomial underflows. Clamp input to [-87.33, 88.72] before polynomial evaluation.
Discovered while debugging softmax NaN in the RVV attention path.

After investigation, we discovered the backend was not accounting for aot caching time, s

vllm.title: [XPU] disable fusion pattern support on XPU platform
vllm.author: chaojun-zhang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
disable fusion pattern support on XPU platform.
When the IR priority is set to xpu_kernels, certain fusion patterns (such as the rms_norm fuse pattern) are automatically enabled. However, because XPU does not yet support the rms_norm fuse pattern pass, the system will encounter error if fuse

vllm.title: [TurboQuant] enable FA3/FA4 for prefill paths
vllm.author: huangzhilin-hzl
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Resolves part of #40069 (Backend Coverage: extend flash_attn_varlen_func support to FA3/4).
Two issues fixed:


FA version passthrough: TurboQuant prefill paths call flash_attn_varlen_func without the fa_version kwarg, so on Hopper (SM90) the call defaults to FA2 instead of leveraging FA3, a

see discussion here #35326 (comment)

vllm.title: [compile] Skip FX graph deserialiaztion on loading, further reducing warm compile time.
vllm.author: zhxchen17
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Following up #38657, we utilize the recently-added Python execution code as the source of truth
to run compiled function moving forward. This means it's not required to serialize the split FX graph anymore, which brings the
warm compile time further down to sub 2 second level.

Be
